title: Vic Tayback

[Forest Lawn Memorial Park](/wiki/Forest_Lawn_Memorial_Park_(Hollywood_Hills) "Forest Lawn Memorial Park (Hollywood Hills)")
*[Alice](/wiki/Alice_(American_TV_series) "Alice (American TV series)")* *[The Love Boat](/wiki/The_Love_Boat "The Love Boat")*

(January 6, 1930 – May 25, 1990) was an American actor. He was best known for his role as diner owner Mel Sharples on the television sitcom *[Alice](/wiki/Alice_(American_TV_series) "Alice (American TV series)")* (1976–1985), as well as his multiple guest appearances on *[The Love Boat](/wiki/The_Love_Boat "The Love Boat")* (1977–1987). The former earned him two consecutive [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards") (for [Best Supporting Actor in a Television Series](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actor_%E2%80%93_Series,_Miniseries_or_Television_Film "Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film")) and a [Primetime Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award "Primetime Emmy Award") nomination for [Outstanding Continuing Performance by a Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Supporting_Actor_in_a_Comedy_Series "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series").

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor (1930-1990)

| Vic Tayback | |
| --- | --- |

**Victor Tayback** (January 6, 1930 – May 25, 1990) was an American actor. He was best known for his role as diner owner Mel Sharples on the television sitcom *[Alice](/wiki/Alice_(American_TV_series) "Alice (American TV series)")* (1976–1985), as well as his multiple guest appearances on *[The Love Boat](/wiki/The_Love_Boat "The Love Boat")* (1977–1987). The former earned him two consecutive [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards") (for [Best Supporting Actor in a Television Series](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Award_for_Best_Supporting_Actor_%E2%80%93_Series,_Miniseries_or_Television_Film "Golden Globe Award for Best Supporting Actor – Series, Miniseries or Television Film")) and a [Primetime Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award "Primetime Emmy Award") nomination for [Outstanding Continuing Performance by a Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award_for_Outstanding_Supporting_Actor_in_a_Comedy_Series "Primetime Emmy Award for Outstanding Supporting Actor in a Comedy Series").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vic_Tayback&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Tayback was born on January 6, 1930 in [Brooklyn, New York](/wiki/Brooklyn,_New_York "Brooklyn, New York"), to [Syrian](/wiki/Syrian_Americans "Syrian Americans") Catholic immigrant parents Helen (née Hanood; [Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): هيلين هنود تايباك) and Najeeb James Tayback ([Arabic](/wiki/Arabic_language "Arabic language"): نجيب جيمس طيبك) . Both of his parents had emigrated to the United States from [Aleppo](/wiki/Aleppo "Aleppo").[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2) He moved with his family to [Burbank](/wiki/Burbank,_California "Burbank, California"), California during his teenage years and attended [Burbank High School](/wiki/Burbank_High_School_(Burbank,_California) "Burbank High School (Burbank, California)"), from which he graduated in 1948.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) He also attended [Glendale Community College](/wiki/Glendale_Community_College_(California) "Glendale Community College (California)") and the Frederick A. Speare School of Radio and TV Broadcasting.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vic_Tayback&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Tayback served in the [United States Navy](/wiki/United_States_Navy "United States Navy") before beginning his acting career at the age of 25. A lifetime member of the [Actors Studio](/wiki/Actors_Studio "Actors Studio"),[[6]](#cite_note-6) he was a familiar face on television in the 1960s and 1970s, appearing on numerous series, including *[The Man from U.N.C.L.E.](/wiki/The_Man_from_U.N.C.L.E. "The Man from U.N.C.L.E.")*, *[Star Trek](/wiki/Star_Trek:_The_Original_Series "Star Trek: The Original Series")*, *[Bonanza](/wiki/Bonanza "Bonanza")*, *[Here Come the Brides](/wiki/Here_Come_the_Brides "Here Come the Brides")*, *[That Girl](/wiki/That_Girl "That Girl")*, *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*, *[The Mary Tyler Moore Show](/wiki/The_Mary_Tyler_Moore_Show "The Mary Tyler Moore Show")*, *[The Partridge Family](/wiki/The_Partridge_Family "The Partridge Family")*, *[All in the Family](/wiki/All_in_the_Family "All in the Family")*, *[Fantasy Island](/wiki/Fantasy_Island "Fantasy Island")*, *[Columbo](/wiki/Columbo "Columbo")*, [Bewitched](/wiki/Bewitched "Bewitched") and *[The Love Boat](/wiki/The_Love_Boat "The Love Boat")*. He also appeared in many films such as *[Bullitt](/wiki/Bullitt "Bullitt")* (1968), *[Papillon](/wiki/Papillon_(1973_film) "Papillon (1973 film)")* (1973), *[The Gambler](/wiki/The_Gambler_(1974_film) "The Gambler (1974 film)")* (1974), *[The Cheap Detective](/wiki/The_Cheap_Detective "The Cheap Detective")* (1978), and *[All Dogs Go to Heaven](/wiki/All_Dogs_Go_to_Heaven "All Dogs Go to Heaven")* (1989), as well as more than 25 stage productions, including *[Twelve Angry Men](/wiki/Twelve_Angry_Men_(1964_play) "Twelve Angry Men (1964 play)")*, *[The Diary of Anne Frank](/wiki/The_Diary_of_Anne_Frank_(play) "The Diary of Anne Frank (play)")*, *[Death of a Salesman](/wiki/Death_of_a_Salesman "Death of a Salesman")*, and *[Brighton Beach Memoirs](/wiki/Brighton_Beach_Memoirs "Brighton Beach Memoirs")*. He was the co-founder of the theatre troupe [Company of Angels](/wiki/Company_of_Angels "Company of Angels"). He also appeared in television commercials, notably in 1968 with [Richard Dreyfuss](/wiki/Richard_Dreyfuss "Richard Dreyfuss") for the [AMC Javelin](/wiki/AMC_Javelin "AMC Javelin").

Tayback's most famous role was as diner owner Mel Sharples in both the film *[Alice Doesn't Live Here Anymore](/wiki/Alice_Doesn%27t_Live_Here_Anymore "Alice Doesn't Live Here Anymore")* (1974) and the television series *[Alice](/wiki/Alice_(American_TV_series) "Alice (American TV series)")* (1976–1985). He was the only actor in the original film to reprise his role for the rest of the series.[[7]](#cite_note-7) For the role, he was nominated for a [Primetime Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Award "Primetime Emmy Award") in 1978 and won two consecutive [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards") in 1980 and 1981. In 1971, he made a guest appearance as Dirks in season 16, episode 19 of the long-running [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS") Western series *[Gunsmoke](/wiki/Gunsmoke "Gunsmoke")*.

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vic_Tayback&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Personal life")]

Tayback was married to Sheila Maureen Barnard (1932–2001) from March 16, 1963, until his death on May 25, 1990. They had one son.

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vic_Tayback&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Death")]

Tayback died of a [heart attack](/wiki/Myocardial_infarction "Myocardial infarction") at age 60 on May 25, 1990.[[8]](#cite_note-LATIMES-8)[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) Tayback was pronounced dead at 1:56 a.m. at Glendale Adventist Medical Center, where he was taken after his wife called paramedics. She said he had climbed out of bed, taken a few steps and collapsed.[[8]](#cite_note-LATIMES-8) Tayback was a lifelong smoker and had a history of heart problems. He underwent a triple bypass in 1983.[[8]](#cite_note-LATIMES-8)

He is buried at [Forest Lawn Memorial Park (Hollywood Hills)](/wiki/Forest_Lawn_Memorial_Park_(Hollywood_Hills) "Forest Lawn Memorial Park (Hollywood Hills)").

## Filmography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Vic_Tayback&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Filmography")]

### Television

* *[All in the Family](/wiki/All_in_the_Family "All in the Family")* (episode: "Et Tu, Archie?") (1974) – Joe Tucker
* *[Barney Miller](/wiki/Barney_Miller "Barney Miller")* (episodes: "Stakeout", "The Social Worker") (1975) – Mr. Savocheck / Master Forger
* *[The Practice](/wiki/The_Practice_(1976_TV_series) "The Practice (1976 TV series)")* (episode: "The Choice") (1976) – Frankie Nyles
* *[Mary Hartman, Mary Hartman](/wiki/Mary_Hartman,_Mary_Hartman "Mary Hartman, Mary Hartman")* (episode 29, season 1) (1976)
* *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")* (episodes: "Bones of Contention"; "Angel in Blue") (1975–1978) – Martin Lynch / Parmel
* *[The Eddie Capra Mysteries](/wiki/The_Eddie_Capra_Mysteries "The Eddie Capra Mysteries")* (episode: "How Do I Kill Thee?") (1978) – Logan
* *Getting Married* (1978) – Burt Carboni
* *[$weepstake$](/wiki/Sweepstakes_(TV_series) "Sweepstakes (TV series)")* (episode: "Billy, Wally and Ludmilla, and Theodore") (1979) – Sergeant Yarbrough
* *[Flo](/wiki/Flo_(TV_series) "Flo (TV series)")* (episode: "What Are Friends For?") (1981) – Mel Sharples
* *[Fantasy Island](/wiki/Fantasy_Island "Fantasy Island")* (1979–1983) – George Walters / Laura Walters / Norman Atkins / Chet Nolan / Melvyn Mews
* *[T. J. Hooker](/wiki/T._J._Hooker "T. J. Hooker")* (episode: "Hooker's War") (1982) – Lieutenant Pete Benedict
* *[Hotel](/wiki/Hotel_(American_TV_series) "Hotel (American TV series)")* (episode: "Relative Loss") (1983) – Wallace Egan
* *[Finder of Lost Loves](/wiki/Finder_of_Lost_Loves "Finder of Lost Loves")* (episode: "Maxwell Ltd: Finder of Lost Loves Pilot") (1984) – Thomas Velasco
* *[Murder, She Wrote](/wiki/Murder,_She_Wrote "Murder, She Wrote")* (episode: "One Good Bid Deserves a Murder") (1986) – Sal Domino
* *[Crazy Like a Fox](/wiki/Crazy_like_a_Fox_(TV_series) "Crazy like a Fox (TV series)")* (episode: "The Duke Is Dead") (1986) – Pete Ross
* *[The Love Boat](/wiki/The_Love_Boat "The Love Boat")* (1977–1987) – Himself / Jack Hamilton / Shelley Sommers / 'Dutch' Boden / Sergeant Harry Beluski / Harry Stewart
* *[Adderly](/wiki/Adderly "Adderly")* (1987) – Gregorin
* *[Tales from the Darkside](/wiki/Tales_from_the_Darkside "Tales from the Darkside")* (episodes: "The New Man" (as Alan Coombs), Season 1, 1984, and "Basher Malone" (as Tippy Ryan), Season 4, 1988)
* *[MacGyver](/wiki/MacGyver_(1985_TV_series) "MacGyver (1985 TV series)")* (1990, episode: "Jenny's Chance") – George Henderson (final television appearance)
